question: While migrating a self-managed Spring SDK app you find `camunda.client.identity.audience` and `camunda.client.identity.scope` in the config. Which replacements does the Camunda 8.8 reference prescribe, and why?

options.text: `camunda.client.auth.audience` and `camunda.client.auth.scope` — Identity is now part of Camunda, so auth settings live under `camunda.client.auth`.
`camunda.client.identity.token-audience` and `camunda.client.identity.token-scope` — only the property suffix changes.
No replacement is needed; both properties still work because Identity remains a separate component.
`camunda.client.cloud.audience` and `camunda.client.cloud.scope` — Identity values now belong to the cloud namespace.

a.text: The deprecation table for `camunda.client.identity` maps `audience` to `camunda.client.auth.audience` and `scope` to `camunda.client.auth.scope`, with the note that Identity is now part of Camunda.

b.text: There is no `identity.token-audience` or `identity.token-scope` namespace in the reference; the identity namespace as a whole is deprecated.

c.text: These properties are explicitly listed as deprecated. The replacement namespace is `camunda.client.auth`, not `camunda.client.identity`.

d.text: `camunda.client.cloud.*` carries SaaS cluster connection details (domain, region, cluster id), not OAuth audience or scope.

explanation: Because Identity is now part of Camunda, OAuth client-credentials properties move out of `camunda.client.identity.*` and into the unified `camunda.client.auth.*` namespace. `audience` and `scope` keep their leaf names and pair with the rest of the auth settings (token URL, client id, client secret).

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter - properties reference
